scenario:
---
name: competitor-snapshot
description: "E2E: Competitor Snapshot module with real Apify actor calls"
abortOnFailure: false
---

## Test
Use the competitive-intel skill. I'm a Product Marketing Manager at Apify. Give me a competitor snapshot of Oxylabs — positioning, pricing, strengths, vulnerabilities. Work autonomously.

## Checkpoint

### Checks
contains: Oxylabs
warn-regex: https?://
jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("apify (actors )?call"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("--user-agent"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("google-search-scraper"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("website-content-crawler"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("Linkedin-Company-Scraper"; "i"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("wayback-machine-scraper"))] | length > 0
warn-regex: \[(?:Confidence:\s*)?(?:\*+|_+)?(?:High|Medium|Low|V|C|E|\?)\b[^\[\]]*(?:\||[—–\-]|\](?![a-zA-Z]))
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Skill")] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("apify (actors )?call")) | select(test("--json"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Read") | .input.file_path? // "" | select(test("reference/modules/competitor-snapshot"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("apify info"))] | length > 0

### Script
INPUT=$(cat)
LINES=$(echo "$INPUT" | wc -l)
URLS=$(echo "$INPUT" | grep -oE 'https?://[^ )]+' | wc -l)
if [ "$LINES" -lt 60 ]; then echo "FAIL: report too short ($LINES lines, need 60+)"; exit 1; fi
if [ "$URLS" -lt 8 ]; then echo "FAIL: not enough source URLs ($URLS, need 8+)"; exit 1; fi
echo "PASS: $LINES lines, $URLS URLs"

### Judge
You are evaluating a competitive intelligence report. You have two jobs:
1. Grade the output against the criteria below
2. Critique YOUR OWN evaluation criteria (report in eval_critique field)

## Step 1: Gather Evidence

Use your tools to inspect what actually happened:
1. Check `eval-datasets/` directory — contains Apify actor run results downloaded by the runner
2. Also check `/tmp/*.json` — data files the agent may have saved locally
3. These are the actual scraped data for grounding verification
3. Read `skills/apify-easy-competitive-intelligence/reference/verification-checklist.md`

## Step 2: Evaluate

### A: Prompt Coverage

The user prompt asked for a competitor snapshot of Oxylabs. User-asked questions:
- Positioning
- Pricing
- Strengths
- Vulnerabilities

Apply this standard to EACH question above:
- PASS only if substantively answered using /tmp/*.json data — verbatim quotes, counts, named entities, or URLs traceable to scraped files.
- PASS if explicitly answered with grounded absence: report must name (i) which sources were checked, (ii) what was searched for, (iii) what came back.
- FAIL if silently omitted from report.
- FAIL if answered only with generic claims not traceable to eval-datasets/*.json or /tmp/*.json.
- FAIL if agent had scraped data containing the answer but didn't surface it.

Section A overall: PASS only if EVERY question scores PASS.

### B: Data Grounding

Pick 3-5 of the most specific claims in the report (a number, rating, price, quote, or date). For each:
1. Quote the exact claim from the report
2. Find backing evidence in eval-datasets/*.json or /tmp/*.json files
3. Mark as GROUNDED (found in scraped data) or UNGROUNDED (not found)

Rules:
- ANY claim UNGROUNDED → FAIL section B
- No data files exist (neither eval-datasets/ nor /tmp/*.json) but report contains specific data → FAIL (likely hallucinated)
- Generic claims without source URLs are suspect — verify at least one

### C: Skill Compliance

Read the verification checklist, then check:

Universal checks:
- [ ] Confidence labels: every major finding has a confidence label in one of these formats: [Confidence: X | Source], [X | Source], or pricing-short [V]/[C]/[E]/[?] with adjacent source. Cite ≥1 example with section reference. Major findings without labels → FAIL.
- [ ] Source URLs: every factual claim has a full https:// URL traceable to eval-datasets/*.json or /tmp/*.json or to a public source page cited inline. Partial, relative, or missing URLs on major claims → FAIL.
- [ ] Inferences labeled: claims that are agent interpretation (not direct from scraped data) use hedging language ("this suggests...", "likely indicates...", "may signal..."). Inferences stated as fact without hedge → FAIL.
- [ ] Failure reporting: every actor call that returned 0 items, errored, or was skipped is explicitly noted in the report — which actor, what input, what came back. Silent skip or unreported empty result → FAIL.

Framework:
- [ ] SWOT (or named equivalent competitive framework) applied with ≥2 entries per quadrant (Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats), each backed by /tmp/*.json data. Single-entry quadrants or label-only without analysis → FAIL.

Section C overall: PASS only if EVERY check above passes.

## Step 3: Verdict

Structure your reasoning field exactly as:

## A: Prompt Coverage
[for each criterion: PASS/FAIL with cited evidence]
Overall: PASS/FAIL

## B: Data Grounding
[for each checked claim: "quote" → source file → GROUNDED/UNGROUNDED]
Overall: PASS/FAIL

## C: Skill Compliance
[for each criterion: status with example or absence]
Overall: PASS/FAIL

## Verdict: PASS/FAIL — [one-sentence reason]

Rules:
- PASS only if A + B + C all pass
- FAIL if any section fails
- When uncertain: FAIL (burden of proof is on the report)
- No partial credit

## Step 4: Eval Critique (eval_critique field)

Consider whether YOUR evaluation criteria (sections A, B, C above) have gaps:
- A criterion that would pass even for a clearly bad report
- An important outcome you observed that no criterion above covers
- A criterion that cannot be verified from the available files
If the criteria are solid, say so briefly.

### warn-Judge
You are reviewing the quality of the evaluation framework for this competitive intelligence scenario.

Read these workspace files first:
1. `eval-checkpoint.json` — all deterministic check definitions (type, pattern, severity)
2. `eval-check-results.json` — pass/fail results for each deterministic check
3. `skills/apify-easy-competitive-intelligence/SKILL.md` — what the skill requires the agent to do

Then evaluate the evaluation framework:
1. Are there important skill requirements that no check (deterministic or judge) covers?
2. Are any deterministic checks too weak — would pass even for clearly bad output?
3. Are any checks redundant (testing the same thing)?
4. Is the FAIL vs WARNING severity split appropriate?
5. Did any check pass that probably shouldn’t have, given what you see in the agent output?
6. Did any check fail/warn that seems unfair or overly strict?

Be specific — cite the check definition from eval-checkpoint.json and the result from eval-check-results.json.
Verdict: pass if framework is solid, fail if significant gaps found.
initBashScript:
set -e
WORKSPACE=$(find /tmp -type d -name 'eval-workspace-*' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
cd "$WORKSPACE"
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/chocholous/apify-easy-competitive-intelligence.git _skill
cp -r _skill/.claude-plugin .claude-plugin
cp -r _skill/skills skills
rm -rf _skill
apify login --token "$APIFY_TOKEN" 2>/dev/null || true
echo "Plugin injected, apify authenticated"
